activities_projects.title: Reciting with Rhythm
activities_projects.objective: Practice oral fluency and expression through poetry
steps: Read the poem 'Try Again' silently to understand the flow
Read it aloud, focusing on the rhyming words at the end of each line
Recite the first stanza for the class with appropriate pauses
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve pronunciation and rhythmic reading
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of speech and rhythmic accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Finding the Main Lesson
activities_projects.objective: Identify the central theme of the poem
steps: Read the poem and find the most repeated phrase
Write down what you think the poet is trying to tell us in one simple sentence
Share your sentence with your bench partner
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students grasp the basic message of perseverance
activities_projects.evaluation: Ability to summarize the theme accurately (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Rhyme Time
steps: Go through the poem and find 4 pairs of rhyming words (e.g., 'fail' and 'prevail')
Write them down in your notebook
Think of one more word that rhymes with each pair
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize phonetic patterns in poetry
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of rhyming words (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Try Again' Poster
steps: Think of a situation where someone might need to 'try again' (like sports or drawing)
Draw a simple picture representing that situation
Write the words 'Try Again!' in big, bold letters on your poster
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect the poem's theme to visual art
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relevance to the theme (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Hunt
activities_projects.objective: Learn the meaning of key words in the poem
steps: Find the words 'Heed', 'Prevail', and 'Strive' in the poem
Look up their simple meanings in the dictionary
Write a short sentence for each word
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct meanings and simple sentence construction (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Success Stories
activities_projects.objective: Relate the poem to real-life experiences
steps: Think of a time you failed at something but kept trying until you succeeded
Write 3 sentences about what happened
Include how you felt when you finally succeeded
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and relevance of the personal story (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Opposite Actions
activities_projects.objective: Understand antonyms in the context of the poem
steps: Find the words 'Success', 'Win', and 'Heed' in the text
Write down the opposite (antonym) for each word
Explain why the poet prefers the positive words over the negatives
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand word relationships and poet's intent

activities_projects.title: Advice Column
steps: Imagine your friend is sad because they lost a race
Write 2-3 sentences of advice using ideas from the poem
Start your advice with: 'Don't be discouraged because...'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to apply poetic themes to social interactions
activities_projects.evaluation: Tone of encouragement and use of poem's logic (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Identify Stanzas
activities_projects.objective: Understand the structure of a poem
steps: Count how many stanzas are in the poem
Count how many lines are in each stanza
Write down which stanza is your favorite and why
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in counting and simple reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Try Again' Slogan
steps: Create a short slogan (max 5 words) based on the poem
Write it on a small strip of paper to use as a bookmark
Decorate it with a small pattern
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to condense information creatively
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and brevity of the slogan (10 marks)
